headline: Uganda's Victory Against Ebola Offers Blueprint for Global Fight
meta_description: Uganda discharged its last Ebola patient, demonstrating how early detection and strong health systems can defeat the deadly virus.
summary_short: Uganda has no active Ebola cases after discharging its final patient. The country's success contrasts sharply with the worsening crisis in neighbouring Democratic Republ…
body:
Uganda has become Ebola-free after discharging its last patient from hospital, marking the end of an outbreak that began in May when a man travelled from the neighbouring Democratic Republic of Congo for treatment.

Health Minister Dr Chris Baryomunsi called the moment "a moment of joy," underscoring what Uganda's experience reveals: that Ebola can be defeated with the right tools. The country recorded just 20 confirmed cases and two deaths—a stark contrast to the escalating crisis across the border, where more than 2,000 confirmed infections and 796 deaths have been recorded.

Uganda's success reflects decades of hard-won experience. Having faced multiple Ebola outbreaks over the last two decades, the country has established robust protocols: rapid isolation of suspected cases, contact tracing, and targeted public health messaging. When symptoms like fever, vomiting, or unexplained bleeding emerged, patients received prompt treatment.

But the broader picture is troubling. The WHO describes the Congo outbreak as the world's third-largest on record, spreading at alarming speed. About two-thirds of deaths occur in communities where people never reach health facilities. The WHO requires $115 million to respond adequately—so far, it has received just $45 million.

Uganda now enters a critical 42-day countdown before official declaration as Ebola-free, provided no new cases emerge. The health ministry's simple message to citizens remains vital: stay vigilant.
